title: Quasi-linearity → Leaky quasi-linearity + strategy cost

perturbation.original: Each agent has quasi-linear utility over money: u_i(x_i, t_i) = v_i(x_i) - t_i, where t_i is payment.
perturbation.modified: Each agent has leaky quasi-linear utility with strategy cost: u_i(x_i, t_i, s_i) = v_i(x_i) - t_i - c(s_i), where s_i is the descriptive complexity of the strategy and c(·) is a non-decreasing cost function. For zero cost (truth-telling is the default), the original form is recovered.

scores.reasoning: Pareto-dominating allocations are still preferred by agents, but strategy costs may make some Pareto-improving outcomes unattainable when they require complex strategies.
With strategy cost, the 'simple' rule becomes a meaningful object of choice; users can understand 'this rule is simple, that one isn't'.
Truth-telling is no longer dominant when it requires more descriptive effort than strategic manipulation.
Computational feasibility is orthogonal to strategy cost, but constrains c(·) to be computable.

new_axiom_candidate.statement_nl: Axiom of Bounded Strategy Description (BSD): each agent's strategy s_i is drawn from a description-bounded set S_i ⊂ Σ*, and the cost c(s) is proportional to the Kolmogorov complexity K(s).
new_axiom_candidate.formalization: Let Σ be a finite alphabet. For each agent i, fix a budget b_i ∈ ℝ+ and define the feasible strategy set S_i = {s ∈ Σ* : K(s) ≤ b_i}. An equilibrium is a strategy profile (s_1, ..., s_n) such that no agent can reduce their cost c(s_i) (or increase their utility) by deviating to any s'_i ∈ S_i.
new_axiom_candidate.justification: BSD makes 'simplicity' a first-class citizen. It is a punctual, model-specific axiom that combines robustness_strategic (with cost) and robustness_perspective (with description length).
falsifiable_predictions: Mechanisms whose truthful strategies have high Kolmogorov complexity will see lower truth-telling rates in experiments (cf. Thaler-Sunstein nudge literature).
Optimal auctions under BSD are not Myerson-style; they penalize complex bid formats.

consequences.statement: Revenue Equivalence Theorem no longer holds: two mechanisms with the same efficient allocation but different strategy complexity can yield different expected revenue, because agents may select different strategies under identical allocations.
Description-length tax: in BSD settings, simple posted-price mechanisms may dominate complex VCG mechanisms despite VCG's theoretical optimality under full rationality.
Mechanism design under description complexity becomes a problem of compression: the designer chooses a code (description scheme) for strategies, and agents optimize within that code.

derivation_chain: Original: A3-ql (quasi-linear) + A1-bic (BIC) → Myerson optimal auction
Now A1-bic is violated → A1-bic' (BIC under BSD) is the new strategic axiom
Theorem T-revenue-eq: needs rederivation → parametric form R_eq(λ) where λ measures description cost weight
Theorem T-optimal-auction: rederives to a complexity-penalized allocation rule
BSD is the new axiom; A3-ql is replaced; A1-bic is replaced; A2-ir survives unchanged
